mechanism: Deadline compression / longshot premium on near-dated threshold binaries: as the hourly settlement approaches, the remaining-move distribution narrows faster than the quoted ATM threshold converges, so the nearest-to-money '$X or above' leg keeps too much probability mass on a late cross. Sellers of the ATM threshold in the last minutes collect the premium.
signal_desc: First bar with time-to-close in [2,6) minutes, quote fresh (mid stale <= 2 min) and finite implied mid. No feature, no quote-level condition.
direction: SELL (side='under') the ATM-snapped threshold at i+1 (1-min latency, ATM re-snap at fill), hold to settlement. FillModel(venue=kalshi, half_spread=0.015).

results.verdict_reason: late-window ATM sell premium is real GROSS (+2.3 to +2.7c, consistent sign train/nontest) but below honest single-leg execution cost (1.5c half-spread + ~1.7c Kalshi taker fee at a ~0.58 fill); net negative on BOTH splits, bootstrap CI spans 0, gate failed

mechanism: Favorite-longshot bias in the final minutes of Kalshi hourly crypto threshold ladders: late flow overpays for cheap tail YES legs (lottery demand on a 'one more push' cross) and underprices near-certain upper favorites, leaving a taker-collectable premium net of fees. PRE-VERIFIED candidate: adversarial verification round codex_r2_fav_tail (memo preserved at research/lab/runs/fable_crypto2_favtail_verification_memo_codex_r2.md) WEAKENED the broad scout rule and froze this exact capped taker spec; memo dev-panel expectation +3.24c/contract (n=587, 492 events, cluster SE 0.93c, t=3.48), survives 2x fee stress (+2.72c).
signal_desc: Exact minute candles relative to settlement close (close_ts = event date at hour:00 America/New_York == memo's date+(hour+4)h over this all-EDT sample; candle quotes are minute-close). Eligible thresholds: exact entry-minute candle present, required side quote non-missing, non-crossed (yes_ask >= yes_bid when both exist), candle volume > 0 that minute. LEG A at T-15: yes_ask in [0.70, 0.75]. LEG B at T-5: yes_bid in [0.02, 0.08]. Max one threshold per event per leg; favorite pick = ask closest to 0.72 (tie lower ask, then lower boundary), longshot pick = highest bid (tie lexical ticker).
direction: TWO legs under this one hypothesis, each one-trade-per-event, hold to settlement; the DECISIVE gate object is the COMBINED trade set (per-leg gates are diagnostics; the favorite leg alone cannot clear the n>=200 floor). LEG A: BUY YES (side='over') the selected 70-75c favorite at T-15. LEG B: SELL YES / buy NO (side='under') the selected 2-8c longshot at T-5. Honest i+1: fill 1 minute after the signal with the strike PINNED to the signal's selection. FillModel(venue='kalshi', half_spread=0.015).

provenance.pipeline: scout wave -> adversarial verification -> this lane
provenance.memo_dev_panel: nontest dev panel = 879 locked train+val ids (memo numbers are in-sample on that panel)

gate.verdict_reason: memo's frozen taker spec reproduced EXACTLY (locked-panel trade set 587 trades / 492 events == memo's frozen-spec counts; lane net there +1.67c vs memo +3.24c, gap == the pre-declared harsher execution: candle-mid + flat 1.5c half-spread + per-order-ceiling Kalshi fee vs at-the-touch + unrounded fee). The favorite-longshot premium is real gross (combined gross +5.0c train / +4.5c nontest; favorite leg net +7.6c/+6.4c with bootstrap CI lo > 0) but the DECISIVE combined gate FAILS on BOTH splits: season-split late-half decay (walkforward Apr +3.3c -> May +0.9c -> Jun n=3 -47.5c; the memo's pre-flagged May-compression and June failure modes materialized) and parity-split instability; the longshot leg (75% of trades) is ~zero net under lane taker execution (+0.3-0.5c, CI spans 0, May negative); the favorite leg alone is structurally n-starved (125-151 trades < 200 floor). DEAD as a taker strategy under lane execution discipline; the memo's maker variant (+3.59c conditional on fill) is the natural successor hypothesis.
